description: Public opinion regarding the demand for federal intervention and accountability following the discovery of lead-contaminated water in Flint, Michigan. This period tracks the shift from local skepticism to national outrage between January and June 2016.

key_events.description: President Obama declares a federal state of emergency in Flint.
CNN hosts a Democratic Town Hall in Flint elevating the crisis to a national debate.
Congressional hearings begin with testimony from EPA and state officials.
Governor Rick Snyder apologizes under intense public pressure during testimony.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how rapidly localized environmental health crises mobilize national political sentiment.
